mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales indicate strengthening consumer demand that translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or the positive return hit rate is not significantly above 50% (binomial p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate above 4.5% reflects restrictive monetary policy that raises discount rates and borrowing costs, suppressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 55% of triggered months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint increases in industrial production and decreases in unemployment signal a non-inflationary supply expansion where output growth absorbs labor slack without wage pressure, boosting earnings expectations and producing positive 60-day equity returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term generates a Rank IC below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or the signal underperforms a simple INDPRO-only model.

mechanism_narrative: During monetary tightening cycles, a falling unemployment rate signals an overheating labor market that forces the Federal Reserve to hike rates further, tightening financial conditions and producing negative S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, declining UNRATE fails to predict negative 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.10 versus zero or positive returns).

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in industrial production reflects an imminent inflection in manufacturing activity and inventory investment, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent week as markets price in earnings momentum.
failure_condition: Positive predictive power disappears with a 5-day Rank IC below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts collapsing below 600 thousand units indicates a severe contraction in residential investment and downstream durable consumption, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as growth expectations are revised down.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are non-negative in more than 50% of instances when HOUST is below 600 over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained upward momentum in the consumer price index erodes real corporate margins and triggers tighter monetary policy expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon as valuation multiples contract.
failure_condition: Rank IC turns positive over any 3-year rolling window, indicating inflation momentum no longer predicts negative equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP captures aggregate demand outperformance relative to prior-period expectations, and positive surprises propagate into equity prices over the subsequent quarter, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: GDP changes show no significant correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.
